review_notes: The functions provided are mostly accurate and map well to Kubernetes security controls related to boundary protection. However, the function 'k8s_service_type_not_nodeport' could be more descriptive to indicate the security implication of avoiding NodePort services. The naming conventions are followed correctly, and the functions are technically accurate. The functions align with the FedRAMP SC-7 (12) control by focusing on network policies and secure service configurations.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_service_type_not_nodeport' to 'k8s_service_nodeport_disabled' for clarity and consistency with other naming patterns.
title: Boundary Protection | Host-based Protection
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP control SC-7 (12) focuses on boundary protection, particularly host-based protection, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to network security controls. NetworkPolicies in Kubernetes can enforce ingress and egress restrictions, ensuring that pods are not unnecessarily exposed to the internet. Additionally, ensuring services are not using NodePort and that Ingress resources have TLS enabled further aligns with boundary protection by limiting exposure and securing communications.
